text: e-0040/a-0038 gave the single-comparison rough-vs-fine pass@1 MDE and flagged -- but never quantified -- that the CAPSTONE (q-0001) is a gap x model-SIZE INTERACTION (the gap GROWS as the model shrinks), a difference-in-differences (DiD) that is 'strictly harder to power' so those MDEs are a LOWER bound. e-0041 supplies the missing number. Modeling four per-problem outcomes on the SAME held-out theorems ({small,large} model x {fine,rough} policy) with a one-factor Gaussian copula (all pairwise rho=0.5) and a two-sided z-test on the per-problem interaction statistic D=(rough_small-fine_small)-(rough_large-fine_large) -- with type-I error calibrated to 0.042-0.057 across all cells, confirming the test is valid -- the DiD MDE (smallest small-model gap against a ZERO large-model gap at 80% power, s_f in {0.05,0.10,0.20}) is: n=50 +0.25/0.28/0.31; n=63 +0.22/0.24/0.28; n=116 +0.16/0.18/0.20; n=200 +0.12/0.13/0.15. That is ~1.3-1.4x e-0040's single-gap MDE (a +0.04..+0.07 absolute interaction surcharge). At the most affordable supply-ample size n=116 the eval can only flag the interaction for a small-model gap >= +0.16-0.20 absolute -- a huge granularity effect, far above any plausible few-point gap. And it UNDERSTATES the bar: a real positive large-model gap shrinks the DiD to resolve, demanding still more power. DECISION: the headline 'gap grows as the model shrinks' is unresolvable by a single-seed paired pass@1 eval at any affordable n; the Modal go/no-go must either drop the DiD-significance framing for effect-sizes-with-CIs across sizes + pass@k>1 / continuous-margin outcomes + n well past 200, or restrict the paper to a single-size gap (e-0040) rather than the interaction. CAVEAT: design-stage power calc; exchangeable rho=0.5 across all four arms (same-base correlations likely exceed cross-size ones, which would RAISE power -- conservative) and a clean zero large-model gap (optimistic); projects power, not a measured pass@k outcome.
